Experimental Television Center records, 1969-2011

Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Experimental Television Center records, 1969-2011
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
8229x
Creator:
Experimental Television Center
Hocking, Ralph.
Quanitities:
152 cubic feet.
3 mapcase folders.
Scope and content

Collection contains files related to programs and events sponsored or supported by ETC, grant proposals and reports, research files, publications from the ETC library, files on individual artists and institutions, taped interviews, and recorded conferences and workshops.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Preferred Citation

Experimental Television Center records, #8229. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical

The Experimental Television Center (ETC) was founded in Owego, N.Y. in 1971 as an outgrowth of a media access program established by Ralph Hocking at Binghamton University in 1969, to provide support and services to the media arts community. ETC's mission was to encourage the creation of work using new electronic media technologies; to encourage an informed appreciation of media art; and to help honor our independently created moving-image heritage by initiating projects and participating in partnerships which address the needs for research, education and preservation, and place independent works within a larger cultural context.

CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series I. Programs and events
Original arrangement. The folders are organized by program, report, activity, and correspondence, mostly chronological.
Processing Information
Boxes 60 and 61: Folder titles created by staff (Alana Staiti)
Scope and Contents
This series contains promotional materials (flyers, posters, programs, press releases, articles) and planning materials (correspondence, reports, meeting minutes) related to the events that the Experimental Television Center (ETC) and other affiliated institutions (e.g. Visual Studies Workshop) have organized or participated in.
Access to folders in this series must be requested a week in advance.

Correspondence.

ETC Correspondence
 1984-1985
Scope and Contents
Correspondences, articles, flyers. It includes: film and Video Makers Travel Sheets 1985; Book by Ben Britton, "Television in American Culture," 2nd Edition #19/20, 1983; Series of correspondences between Ralph Hocking and state officials regarding NYSCA funding for 1985-1986 state budget; thank-you letter from Jason Homer, January 21-29, 1984(?); Correspondences from Gary Hill; Doris Chase; Issues of Video Networks: Bay Area Video Coalition, 1985; Issues of Changing Channels, 1985; Mailing advertising Shigeko Kubota installation "Niagara Falls," The Kitchen, New York, with note from Kubota, March 1985; Correspondences from Jim Duesing; Program for Stockholm International Video Festival, 1985; correspondence related to the Bronx Museum of the Arts exhibition, "Emerging Expression: The Artist and the Computer,"1985.

ETC Activities
 1985-1986
Scope and Contents
Flyers, posters, press releases, articles. It includes: postcard and program for The 1986 New York City Experimental Video and Film Festival, May-June 1986; Program for "No-TV" #4, Visual Studies Workshop, Rochester, NY, April 15-July 3, 1986; Film and Video Makers Travel Sheet, 1986; Issue of Visions, Vol. 10, No. 3 April/May 1986, Boston Film/Video Foundation; Poster for New Channels, Tonawandas' Council on the Arts, New York, September 1985-January 1986; Flyer for Anthology Video Program, Anthology Film Archives, Winter 1986; Flyer for "Video Viewpoint," the Museum of Modern Art, 1985-1986; Promotional card for "Engendered Species," New Math Gallery, September 17-October 13, 1985(?); List of attendees for Media Alliance 1985 Annual Conference and program; Poster for Mary Ross and Jenni Lukac photography exhibition, Elmira College, September 29-October 19, 1985; Anthology Video Program Spring 1986 from Anthology Film Archives; Poster for Artists as Video/Filmmakers Series, May 1986; article "Video Owego" on ETC, Press and Sun-Bulletin, December 29, 1985.

ETC Activities (oversized) part 2

Events at The Kitchen February/March mailer.
ETC Correspondence
 1986
Scope and Contents
Letter from Clarence D. Rappleyea to Sherry Miller about the successful acquisition of funds for NYSCA fiscal 1986-1987, April 15, 1986; Project description by Peer Bode, discusses his vision, inspiration, and views on electronic imaging, 1986; NYSCA Newsletter, Winter 1986 No. 3, announces Peer Bode's award for frame buffer construction and videotape making through ETC; Flyer from Media Alliance expressing urgent need to advocate for arts funding in New York, 1986, attached to a letter from Deborah Gee-Tritschler, Broome County Arts Council, to area arts organizations, no date; Handwritten letter from Shigeko Kubota to Sherry Miller and Ralph Hocking, mentioning Kubota's diary tape and Nam June Paik, February 20, 1986, and reply letter, presumably from Sherry Miller but not signed, March 17, 1986; Letter from Dai Sil Kim-Gibson, Director of Media Program NYSCA to Sherry Miller, regarding upcoming panel meeting and Minority Development Proposals, January 10, 1986; Correspondences from Reynold Weidenaar, 1985-1986; Handwritten letter from Jeff Bechtel to Sherry Miller, November 28, 1985; Series of correspondences from Sciarabba Walker & Co. Accountants, Ithaca NY to Ralph Hocking regarding audit for fiscal year 1985; Letter from Sherry Miller to Ben Day, Professor in the Department of Communication Arts and Design, Virginia Commonwealth University, regarding Walter Wright's tenure review, October 22, 1985; Letter from Robin F. White, Director of Media Alliance to Ralph Hocking and Sherry Miller regarding annual conference, curated screenings by Shalom Gorewitz, demonstrations by David Jones, August 9, 1985; Letters from S. Sengupta, Director of Sonex Private Limited, Calcutta, India, May 31, 1985, and Alberto Pascual Rodriguez, Production Assistant, Sociedad de Estudios de Radio y Television (SERTEL), postmarked 1985, requesting information about video production and training at ETC; Letter from assemblyman Matthew J. Murphy to Sherry Miller regarding securing additional funding for NYSCA for fiscal year 1986, March 19, 1986; Letter from Shalom Gorewitz to Sherry Miller and Ralph Hocking, regarding current projects using the Amiga and Commodore, undated.

ETC Activities
 1986-1987
Scope and Contents
Correspondences, forms, flyers, articles, press releases. It includes: articles about Connie Coleman and Alan Powell, 1986-1987; Catalog for "G.R.I.D." exhibition, Arnot Art Museum, July 12-September 28, 1986; Flyer for Media Alliance discussion at 1986 Annual Conference, "Addressing Diversity: Minority and Upstate Media," September 18, 1986; Slides of the work of Julius Vitali, 1986; Graphic and correspondence related to Reynold Weidenaar's "The Thundering Scream of the Seraphim's Delight," 1987; Article about Julius Vitali, Studio Photography magazine, May 1987; Excerpts from Computer Graphic magazine about the work of Julius Vitali (in Italian), 1987; Flyer for New York State Youth Media Arts Program, Ithaca Regional Media Arts Show, Ithaca College, March 9, 1987; Flyer for Techno Bop 1987, curated by Sara Hornbacher; Slides of the work of Peter Chamberlain, no date; Program notes for No-TV, January 3-March 22, 1987; Flyer for "Video as Art", Cortland Arts Council Gallery, March 1-27, 1987; Registration form for Upstate Media Conference, May 17, 1987, Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art, Cornell University; Program for Peter D'Agostino's "Double You (and X, Y, Z.)," Philadelphia Museum of Art, April 18-July 5, 1987.

 1972

Film festival posters
 1980
Series II. Proposals and reports
Scope and Contents
This series contains applications, budgets, reports, and correspondence related to granting agencies including New York State Council for the Arts (NYSCA) and the National Endowment for the Arts (NEA). These grants are used primarily for artist residency, program, event, and research. The series also contains materials related to ETC's own residency program, presentation fund, finishing fund, electronic arts grant, etc.
Access to folders in this series must be requested a week in advance.
Proposal for Expansion of the Experimental Television Center, NYSCA
 1970-1971
Scope and Contents
Contents include proposal to NYSCA (New York State Council on the Arts) for the development of Experimental Television Center (Community Center for Television Production), and copy of first contract with payment through WSKG-TV, Binghamton.
Paik, Nam June
Proposal for the Use of Television as an Educational Medium Fall '69
 1969
Scope and Contents
Contents include Ralph Hocking's original proposal for educational television at Binghamton University.

 1969-1971

 1970-1971

 1971-1972

Abe, Shuya

NEA Proposal: Paik, Final Report
 1974-1975
Scope and Contents
Proposal for Nam June Paik's residency at ETC for research on Synthesizer, and proposal for Walter Wright, Ralph Hocking, and David Jones to develop publications about construction and operation of video synthesizers. Nam June Paik's final narrative report references Ralph Hocking's piece, "A Toy for A Toy."

Scope and Contents
"Visions and Voices" Exhibition March 2-16, 1990 – Kathryn High (video artist); 3 copies of "Video Witnesses: February 2-8, 1990" by Hallwalls Contemporary Arts Center; The Brooklyn Museum December 1989 Newsletter; Pamphlet for "Video 101: An Introduction to American Video Art" at the Brooklyn Museum, October 15-November 26, 1989; Booklet on the Brooklyn Museum's Public Programs: October, November, December 1989; Copy of a newspaper article "Video Is Making Waves in the Art World" by Andy Grundberg in Weekend: The New York Times Friday, November 17, 1989; Copy of a newspaper article (continuation of Grundberg article) "Video Installations Are Making Waves in Art" with subheading "Video/Musica" highlighted.

Series III. Research
Scope and Contents
This series includes bibliographies, syllabi, notes, manuscripts, interviews, and other sources related to a variety of research projects by Woody Vasulka, Steina Vasulka, Ralph Hocking, Sherry Miller Hocking, among others.
The subseries National Endowment for the Arts Funded Research Project in Box 117, 27-66; Box 1118, 1-8, consists of research projects supported by the National Endowment for the Arts. Jon Burris is the primary researcher.

Image Processing Manual Xerox Master 3/86 2394
 1986
Scope and Contents
Image Processing Manual (1986). Signal graph leaflets. Information on The Experimental Television Center's Analog Synthesizer. Image Processing Manual; The Analog Control System has detailed explanations of gain, bias, waveshape. Also original Digital Imaging System 2502 The Experimental Television Center's digital system: Z-80 8 bit microprocessor, 7 channels of digital to analog and analog to digital conversion, with dual floppy disk storage and paper print-out. CAT 100 Buffer.

Research Notes

Research notes for History Reclamation Project, Upstate Media Activity 1968-80 including chronology, correspondence, and list of New York State video spaces and artists.
Hocking, Sherry Miller
National Endowment for the Arts Funded Research Project
 1969-1978
Stephen Beck Notes
 1977-1978
Scope and Contents
Interview. The research project concerned the design and creation of electronic moving-image instruments by independent artists and engineers, and the place of this work within the context of video art history. The project included original research, including audio interviews and the creation of original informational and other texts, and the compilation of existing descriptive materials. The notes on the audiotapes were written by Jon Burris, a researcher with the project. Stephen Beck interview.

Vasulka, Steina
Beck, Stephen
Burris, Jon

Series IV: Publications
Scope and Contents
This series contains books, periodicals (newspapers, magazines, journals) and exhibition catalogs (with DVDs) related to video art. The titles include The Independent, Afterimage, Videography, Amazing Computing, etc. The series also contains unpublished manuscripts and relevant correspondence.
Arts Advocate
 1975-DateTo
Scope and Contents
Original journal related to Associated Councils of the Arts on legislation and legal issues; Brochures for Advocates for the Arts.

 1977
Scope and Contents
Original newspapers related to Art Communication.

Series V. Artist Files
Arranged alphabetically in serveral groupings.
Scope and Contents
This series includes documents (resumes, biographies, proposals, manuscripts, artist statements, etc.), artworks, (photographs, negatives, drawings, prints, etc.), and promotional materials (programs, posters, flyers, etc.). Media materials such as digital prints, texts, videos, scanned ephemera are also included.

Bill Etra Scan Processor
 1991
Scope and Contents
Black and white negative. Scan processor(wobbulator) constructed by Bill Etra about 1972 after observing the Nam June Paik machine at the TV Lab at WNET-TV, NYC. Later acquired by Barbara Buckner. Photograph by Dennis Dunda for the "Pioneers of Electronic Art" (Eigenwelt der Apparatewelt) exhibition at Ars Electronica catalog cover.

Van [Evangelos Dousmanis] Negative Book B- TV Cello and Bra
 1971-1972
Scope and Contents
Negatives. Photographs by Evangelos Dousmanis.  1971 8th Annual Avant Garde Festival at The Armory, NYC. Carlota Schoolman; Constance De Jong; Shirley Clarke's Video Ferris Wheel; Ralph Hocking's ETC Living Room; Bob Diamond; Ken Dominick; Sherry Miller Hocking. 1971 Bonino Gallery Nam June Paik exhibition; Charlotte Moorman TV Cello; TV Glasses; Ralph Hocking; John Lennon; Yoko Ono; Ralph Hocking and Nam June Paik on street in Soho; Sherry Miller Hocking at Carlota Schoolman and James Dearing loft Wooster Street. Shuya Abe and ETC Paik Abe Video Synthesizer setup at Bonino Gallery. Paik at ETC; portapak. Sherry Miller Hocking and Ralph Hocking. Everson Museum of Art (Syracuse) Paik and Moorman exhibition, January 18-23, 1972: TV Glasses, TV Cello, Paik Abe Video Synthesizer, with ETC systems. TV Cello construction; Bob Diamond, Ralph Hocking. Charlotte Moorman; Shigeko Kubota; Paik Abe Video Synthesizer.

Bode, Peer
DVD-2648
Rattapallax DVD
 2008
Digital
The digital preservation copy may be made available on request if a delivery copy is not already online.
Scope and Contents
Contents include works by Antonello Faretta, Guilherme Marcondes,Taatske Pieterson, Julian Gray, Kylie Hibbert, Avi Dabach, Abbas Saffari, Caecilia Tripp, Eugene Ostashevsky, Ima Aikio, and Jason Lam. Special features on Billy Collins, John Giorno, Yehuda Amichai, and the death of William Burroughs, award winning animated short films based on poems by William Blake and Sylvia Plath; and a hip-hop rendition of Gertrude Stein's poems by Jean Grae and DJ Spooky, showcase of the winners from the Zebra Poetry Film Festival in Berlin. Support from ETC Presentation Funds.
DVD

Simon Tarr
DVD-2652
Personas and Personalities
 2006
Scope and Contents
Contents include Michael Mittelman short experimental videos and other media art. Each volume has its own theme. Volume 7 is called Personas and Personalities. It contains contributions from ten artists and focuses on the construction of identity and role-playing, along with the influence society and the mass media have on this construction. Aspect: The Chronicle of New Media Art.

Hayashi, Sachiko

Olson, Maria
CD-3507
Conversations with Angels
 Undated
Digital
The digital preservation copy may be made available on request if a delivery copy is not already online.
Scope and Contents
Conversations with Angels is a 3D multiuser Internet project by AMPCOM [now the MEET Factory] (Andy Best and Merja Puustinen) using VRML 2.0 together with Sony's Community Place plugin for Netscape allowing real-time chat between users in a virtual world. Meetfactory and Banff Centre.

Series VII. Discussions and interviews
Scope and Contents
This series contains audio and video tapes of interviews and conversations with various artists. Transcripts and notes are sometimes provided. It also contains ETC's telephone logs (31 spiral notebooks with telephone messages from various artists).
The material in Box 106-A, Experimental Television Center with the Vasulkas, Interviews by Jon Burris was created with a grant from the National Endowment for the Arts. The research project concerns the design and creation of electronic moving-image instruments by independent artists and engineers, and the place of this work within the context of video art history. The researchers include Woody Vasulka, Steina Vasulka, Ralph Hockings, Sherry Miller Hocking, and Jon Burris.
ETC Telephone Logs
 1973-1990
Scope and Contents
Contents include 31 spiral notebooks with telephone messages. Calls from various artists including Gary Hill, Barbara Buckner, Peter Campus, and Nam June Paik.

TR-16948
History of Electronic Sound Modification: Harold Bode
 1981
Scope and Contents
Materials are based on presentations by Harald Bode at the Experimental Television Center and Binghamton University in 1980, and at the Midwest Acoustics Conference, 1981, the Audio Engineering Society, 1981. Manuscript courtesy of Harald Bode and Peer Bode. Published in the Journal of the Audio Engineering Society, October 1984. Published in the Journal of the Audio Engineering Society. TR-16961
Nuts and Volts: McArthur and Wright
 1975-05-06
Scope and Contents
Contents include audiotape of presentations by Don McArthur and Walter Wright at Nuts and Volts conference. The conference presenters include Ton Constanten, Harald Bode, Steve Rutt, Steina Vasulka and Woody Vasulka, Arnold Dreyblatt, Robert Moog, Lajaren Hiller and John Myhill. The concerts participants include Joel Chadabe, Judith Martin, Freida Schrott and the Buffalo String Quartet, Pat Feldballe and Walter Gajewski, Martin Kalve and Friends. The video presenters include Ralph Hocking, Walter Wright, Don McArthur, Jean- Pierre Boyer.
cassette tapes
McArthur, Donald
Wright, Walter
TR-16963
Ralph Hocking Society for Photographic Education Presentation
 1983-03
Scope and Contents
Materials are related to Society for Photographic Education National Conference: Photography Within the New Technology/Defining a New Philosophy of Education. Philadelphia. March 17-20, 1983. The panel is entitled "Defining a NewPhilosophy of Education: Collective Cogitation on New Directions in Education." The speakers include Steina Vasulka, DeeDee Halleck, Ronald Gregory, John Pfahl, Woody Vasulka, Lyn Blumenthal, John Giancola, George Fifield, Tom Porett, Alan Sondheim, Martha Rosler, Ralph Hocking, Nathan Lyons, Jim Pomeroy, Martha Gever, and Catherine Lord.

Audio cassette
V-10627, V-10628
Media Alliance Conference 1994
 1994
Hi 8
Video History: Making Connections Conference
 1998
Scope and Contents
In this subseries, materials are related to the conference Video History: Making Connections at Syracuse University. Lectures and discussions aimed to explore how this new medium of art, activism, and information was developed by artist innovators, video collectives, new technology manufacturers, funders, museum curators, and public television producers.

Buckner, Barbara - IFP 78, various
Sony V-32
Scope and Contents
The order of works is: 1. Grace, 2. Body, 3. Hours, 4. Night, 5. Lamb, 6. Landscape, 7. A Man, 8. Mese 9. The Blue, 10. Gathering In (note: beginning scene should read orange), 11. Axis, 12. Waters, 13. The Men, 14. A Table (note: beginning scene should read dark golden yellow), 15. Mast, 16. Image of the Kingdom, 17. Dominion; Older works: 18. Landscape. 19. Landscape, 20. Jewels of Air, 21. Rose, 22. View from Christabel
